title: 貴方の夢を受け継いで

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Mejiro Bright throws herself into training for the
Satsuki Sho. However...

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Dober
text_block_list.text: I'll pull ahead right from the start! Raaaaah!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Oh! Dober?! Ngh...

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Dober
text_block_list.text:
Yes, I won! I knew if I kept up the pace, I'd be
able to block you from catching up!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: I ran with all my strength, yet it wasn't enough...

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Why, Trainer?
choice_data_list: You're not used to making the race.
You're not used to making the race.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Making the race?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
A runner ought to know how to set and control
the pace of the race.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Most race makers are front runners or pace
chasers. They set the tempo from the start,
creating an advantage for themselves.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
That's not an option for those who surge forward
at the end. If they're not suited to the pace up
front, they're in for a lot of trouble.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
While Mejiro Dober may be capable of setting
the pace, Mejiro Bright is the model example of
those who can't.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
So, does that mean I should mimic Dober's
technique?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
For the most part, an Umamusume's running style
is something they're born with. Trying something
unfitting would be to ignore their natural
strengths.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I have to find a way to make Mejiro Bright
stronger at what she does best.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Dober
text_block_list.text:
Bright'll be running in the Satsuki Sho at
Nakayama, yes? The final stretch there is pretty
short...

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Dober
text_block_list.text:
Her chances are dangerously slim if she continues
running as is, especially if the pace doesn't suit
her.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Dober
text_block_list.text:
It's probably best to think of a specific
counterstrategy. Unfortunately, I'm at a loss for
suggestions.
choice_data_list:
Let's invite a more strategic racer to coach us,
then.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Oh? Did you say... coach?

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
A lot of strategy goes into knowing just when to
time a spurt—even in disadvantaged
circumstances. There are definitely runners with a
knack for it.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Finding that kind of runner to coach Mejiro Bright
and help her develop a strategy for Nakayama
might be what we need.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
The perfect timing... even when at a
disadvantage...

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
Pardon, Trainer. I have a request, if you don't
mind.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
I'd like to learn from someone who's adapted to
their weaknesses, rather than someone who's
good at spurts outright.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text:
I don't believe learning tactics will result in me
becoming tactical. I'd prefer help from someone
who shares the same struggles as me.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Dober
text_block_list.text:
Someone who struggled like you... I see. I guess
that makes sense.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Dober
text_block_list.text:
I think you could learn a lot from an experienced
Umamusume who runs the way you do.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Mejiro Bright's casual response turns out to be
fairly logical.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
So... an Umamusume who surges during the final
stretch, has recognized skill, and also struggled
during the Classics...

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
Thanks for calling, Trainer! I'm totally pumped to
coach Bright!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text: All right, time to get to it, Bright! Let's get swole!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Thank you for agreeing to help, Ryan. ♪

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Mejiro Bright's eyes sparkle. She must be excited
to be coached by her beloved Mejiro Ryan.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
Your ears open, Bright? When they say the last
stretch is short, they're not just talking about
distance.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
Not being aware of your position can slow your
speed. And if you don't push yourself through the
middle, you'll miss the moment to go for the lead.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
I'll call over some friends and we can do a
scrimmage! Sound good to you?

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Understood, Ryan!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Aaaaaah!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
Don't turn so wide at the corners, Bright! You're
better off putting your muscles into finding an
opening on the inside!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Pant, pant... This is raaather difficult, isn't it?

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
Got that right! But if you don't master it, you'll
end up just like I did. I don't want you quitting
before you've made it.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
Yep! I got pounded into the dirt! I wasn't strong
enough back then, so that was part of it too.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
I don't want you going through what I did, Bright.
So don't let up your effort!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: Understood!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I knew Mejiro Ryan would be the perfect coach.
She speaks so frankly about her failure and
experiences.

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
Bright, a Mejiro has never won the Satsuki Sho
before. But...

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Ryan
text_block_list.text:
Don't worry about that! Only worry about running
well. At the end of it, make sure to leave
everything on the track!

text_block_list.name: Mejiro Bright
text_block_list.text: I will, Ryan!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
She's right—this isn't just Bright's attempt at the
Triple Crown, but another attempt on behalf of
the Mejiro family.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
This easygoing Umamusume carries the
expectations of her family on her shoulders, yet
she appears incredibly happy.
